report_name: WebdriverIO Flaw (CVSS 9.8) Allows CI/CD Takeover via Malicious Git Branches

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerability CVE-2026-25244 allows an attacker to inject and execute arbitrary shell commands on a developer machine or CI/CD server by crafting a malicious git branch name that is processed without proper sanitization.

tools.name: WebdriverIO
tools.description: A widely used open-source framework for automating web and mobile application testing. The '@wdio/browserstack-service' package contains the command injection vulnerability CVE-2026-25244.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-25244
vulnerabilities.description: A critical command injection vulnerability in the '@wdio/browserstack-service' package allows for arbitrary command execution when processing a maliciously crafted git branch name. An attacker can include shell commands in the branch name, which are executed by the vulnerable service.

identities.name: WebdriverIO
identities.description: The open-source project that maintains the WebdriverIO testing framework, which was found to have a critical command injection vulnerability.

identities.name: BrowserStack
identities.description: A cloud web and mobile testing platform. The vulnerability exists in the WebdriverIO service integration package for BrowserStack, '@wdio/browserstack-service'.

identities.name: CI/CD build servers
identities.description: A class of systems, such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or GitLab CI, that are high-value targets for this vulnerability, as compromise could lead to a software supply chain attack.

infrastructure.name: Payload Hosting Server
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled server, such as 'evil.com' from the article's example, used to host a malicious shell script payload for download and execution via a tool like curl as part of the command injection attack.

indicators.name: CVE-2026-25244 Payload Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious WebdriverIO Payload Delivery Domain
id: 5b9a1b3c-8d7e-4f6a-9b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to a domain known to host malicious payloads related to the exploitation of CVE-2026-25244 in WebdriverIO.
references:
  - https://dailycybersecurity.com/9-8-severity-alert-malicious-git-branches-can-hijack-your-webdriverio-build-servers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'evil.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network requests to the example attacker domain 'evil.com' mentioned in the context of the WebdriverIO command injection vulnerability CVE-2026-25244.

indicators.name: Suspicious Git Branch Name with Shell Commands
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Git Branch Name with Shell Commands
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4f-3e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects git commands that include branch names containing shell metacharacters, which could indicate an attempt to exploit command injection vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-25244.
references:
  - https://dailycybersecurity.com/9-8-severity-alert-malicious-git-branches-can-hijack-your-webdriverio-build-servers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\git.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ';'
      - '$('
      - '`'
      - '|sh'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of shell metacharacters within git branch names, a technique used to exploit the command injection vulnerability CVE-2026-25244 in WebdriverIO.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Git Branch Name with Shell Commands
